title: Alembic init + autogenerated initial migration applied to Supabase
description: Step 12 of services-to-database migration. Depends on roadmap-db-migration-06/07/08 (ORM model batches) being merged so Base.metadata knows about all 51 tables. Set up Alembic so the ORM models become the source of truth for the schema, replacing the MySQL-flavoured sql/001_schema.sql. Place ALL alembic files under src/internal/db/migrations/ (NOT at repo root — the bot allowlist forbids root edits). Steps the implementer must do, in order: (1) write src/internal/db/migrations/alembic.ini with script_location=src/internal/db/migrations and sqlalchemy.url=driver://placeholder (real URL comes from env in env.py); (2) write env.py that imports Base from src.internal.db, sets target_metadata=Base.metadata, reads DATABASE_URL from os.environ via dotenv (matches existing conftest.py pattern), runs migrations online via context.configure(url=DATABASE_URL, target_metadata=Base.metadata, compare_type=True); (3) copy a stock script.py.mako template from alembic's defaults; (4) USE the alembic CLI inside the worktree: `alembic -c src/internal/db/migrations/alembic.ini revision --autogenerate -m initial` to produce versions/0001_initial_*.py — this autogenerated file IS the deliverable, do not hand-write it; (5) `alembic -c src/internal/db/migrations/alembic.ini upgrade head` — this applies the migration to Supabase via the DATABASE_URL in .env. Add tests/unit/test_migrations.py with: test_alembic_ini_present, test_env_py_uses_base_metadata (parses env.py source, asserts target_metadata=Base.metadata appears), test_initial_revision_exists (versions/ has at least one .py file with both upgrade() and downgrade() defined), test_alembic_upgrade_idempotent (calls subprocess to run `alembic upgrade head`, expects exit 0 even when already at head), test_supabase_has_expected_tables (uses db_session fixture to query information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'; asserts at least 40 of the 51 expected tables are present — gives slack for edge cases).
acceptance: 1) src/internal/db/migrations/alembic.ini exists with script_location set to src/internal/db/migrations. 2) src/internal/db/migrations/env.py imports Base from src.internal.db and sets target_metadata=Base.metadata. 3) At least one autogenerated migration file in src/internal/db/migrations/versions/ defines both upgrade() and downgrade() functions. 4) tests/unit/test_migrations.py contains test_alembic_ini_present, test_env_py_uses_base_metadata, test_initial_revision_exists, test_alembic_upgrade_idempotent, test_supabase_has_expected_tables — all five pass. 5) Running `alembic -c src/internal/db/migrations/alembic.ini upgrade head` against Supabase exits 0 and creates >=40 tables in the public schema (verifiable via `select count(*) from information_schema.tables where table_schema='public'` returning >=40). 6) Net diff under 400 lines (the autogenerated initial revision is the bulk).
